what_it_does: This file is a prescriptive fusion proposal that defines a single Company/OpenWebUI channel concept, maps OWUI channel features onto existing Company channel primitives, diagnoses duplicated channel stores and MCP tool surfaces, and specifies a consolidation plan.
observations: Front matter marks it as type: proposal, area: channels, register: prescriptive, status: unconfirmed, with a verified_live date and source list.

It distinguishes three legitimate Company layers — transport (cc_channels), durable structure (session_channels), cross-instance shared edge (channel_boundary) — and argues the real duplication is at the named-channel store and MCP tool level, not the layering.
It claims two inward named-channel stores exist: cc_channels' JSON-per-channel store and session_channels' append-only channels.jsonl.
It claims two MCP tools overlap on channel management: cc_channel and the channels/channel_act CQRS pair.
It proposes folding cc_channels' store into session_channels, collapsing the MCP surface to one structure tool-pair and one live-messaging tool, and rebasing ops/owui_room.py onto session_channels.
It maps OWUI features (threads, reactions, @model, Socket.IO, webhooks, member roles) onto Company primitives and lists OWUI weak parts not to inherit.
It includes a verification table that distinguishes live-verified Company-side checks from map-sourced OWUI internals.
It notes channel_boundary realtime websocket client is implemented but unwired to session_channels and not live round-tripped.
standouts: The central thesis: an OWUI channel is a rendered face of a single session_channels row, not a separate schema or second home.
The real duplication is identified as two named-channel stores and a doubled MCP management surface, while the transport/structure/boundary layering is deemed legitimate and kept.
Reactions-as-control (🛑/⏸) proven in ops/owui_room.py is proposed for promotion into the channel primitive itself.
channel_boundary realtime is implemented but lacks the discriminator hook that decides which posts cross the boundary.
conventions: Follows the ledger proposal front-matter schema with type, area, posture, register, status, provenance, verified_live, sources, and relates-to.
Cites source files with line-number ranges and uses a verification table with explicit method and result.
Labels claims as VERIFIED LIVE or MAP-SOURCED to distinguish evidentiary basis.
Uses prescriptive register ('the fix', 'must', 'is') despite unconfirmed status.
concerns: Status is unconfirmed but the text reads as a directive consolidation plan.
OWUI donor-side internals are map-sourced from owui-side-map.md and not re-verified in this file, yet used as input to the fusion.
Several proposed additive features (brain-as-member, muted/pinned/last_read_at fields) are described as not yet built.
channel_boundary realtime round-trip and hook are admitted as unverified/unwired, yet the fusion rests on them.
notes: A prescriptive, unconfirmed fusion proposal that wants to collapse two Company channel stores and one OWUI face into a single session_channels-backed channel concept.
questions: Has the proposed store/tool consolidation been approved, and who owns the migration?
What is the data-migration path for existing cc_channels JSON store and owui_room_state.json rosters?
How will the OWUI SvelteKit UI license cap and fork-only nature affect the fusion timeline?
Which product decision will define the shared-vs-internal discriminator for channel_boundary publishing?
purpose_vs_actual: Yes — the stated purpose is to fuse the best parts of OWUI and Company channels into one built-in concept with no duplicates, and the file performs that mapping plus an inward duplication analysis.
intention_signals: The file is trying to turn an external OpenWebUI channel implementation into a native Company channel face by projecting its UX onto existing durable primitives and eliminating parallel roster and tool stores.
novelty: It treats OWUI's relational channel schema as a collapsible projection onto a single append-only Company log, and proposes promoting reaction-based control from a side daemon into a first-class channel event.
contribution: It contributes a unified channel identity model, a feature mapping from OWUI onto Company primitives, a precise diagnosis of duplicated stores and MCP tools, a requirement for surfaced AI-turn failures, and a plan to promote reaction-control semantics into the core channel primitive.
summary_for_embedding: A prescriptive, unconfirmed proposal to fuse OpenWebUI channels with Company channels by treating OWUI as a rendered face of a single session_channels row, consolidating duplicated stores and MCP tools, and mapping OWUI realtime, threads, reactions, and @model onto existing transport, structure, and cognition primitives.
intention_for_embedding: To define a single channel concept built into the Company that absorbs OWUI surface features while eliminating inward and outward duplicate channel stores.
suggested_fields: decision_status — whether each proposed consolidation is approved, deferred, or rejected
verification_scope — live-verified vs map-sourced vs unverified per claim
migration_risk — data-loss/rollover assessment for merging stores
proposed_edge_kinds: renders — OWUI UI/socket renders a session_channels row
projects-onto — OWUI feature maps onto a Company primitive
proposes-consolidation-of — proposal targets a component for merging
